name: Brandywine Transportation Inc
principals: William Gross (Owner); affiliated with Brandywine Bus Sales LLC (Reading, PA)
fleet_size_est: 100-150 (parent group references 250+ vehicles across sales/transport ops; transport contractor portion smaller)
service_types: school (contract pupil transportation; affiliated bus sales arm)
districts_or_customers: Brandywine Heights Area SD, Fleetwood Area SD, Northern Lehigh SD, Southern Lehigh SD (Berks & Lehigh Counties, PA)
recent_news: No public M&A news; remains independent contractor under Brandywine Bus Group umbrella as of 2026; sister entity Brandywine Bus Sales LLC active at 287 W Wesner Rd, Reading PA
website: https://brandywinebus.com/ (sister sales entity; transport contractor has no standalone site)
fit_notes: Multi-district eastern PA contractor with embedded school relationships in Berks/Lehigh counties; family-owned with founder-age owner (W. Gross) raises potential succession angle. Sister bus sales operation could be carve-out vs. integration question. Fits Summit PA tuck-in thesis.

name: Crescent Transit LTD
fleet_size_est: unknown (~20-40 implied by ~50 employees and $2M revenue)
service_types: unclear; listed in Empire State Transportation Workers' Comp Trust (covers school/charter/ambulette operators); Yelp lists Brooklyn location at 1701 Gravesend Neck Rd — likely ambulette/paratransit or small charter rather than confirmed NYC OPT school-bus contractor
districts_or_customers: Brooklyn, NY service area; not listed on NYC DOE OPT contractor roster reviewed; not on NYC school bus operators wiki list
fit_notes: Low-confidence school-bus designation; address profile (Sheepshead Bay storefront) suggests ambulette/paratransit/livery rather than yellow-bus pupil transport contractor. Skip or deprioritize for Summit tuck-in pipeline pending direct confirmation of OPT contract.

name: Bus Maintenance II LLC
owner_or_parent: Logan Bus Company Inc. (affiliate / Logan family group)
fleet_size_est: N/A as standalone — entity sits within Logan Bus & Affiliates which operates ~2,000+ buses across NYC
service_types: school (Logan affiliate); bus maintenance/fleet support arm at 97-14 Atlantic Ave Ozone Park HQ
districts_or_customers: NYC DOE (OPT) school routes via Logan Bus affiliates across Queens, Brooklyn, Bronx, Nassau
recent_news: Logan Bus & Affiliates remains the largest privately owned NY school bus operator (per company materials); ongoing NYC contract activity; no sale announced
fit_notes: NOT an independent tuck-in target. Bus Maintenance II LLC is a Logan Bus affiliate (shared 97-14 Atlantic Ave HQ, Richard Logan principal). Logan Bus is the largest private NY school bus operator (~3,500 employees) — a potential platform-scale combination, not a small tuck-in, and likely uninterested in selling individual affiliates. Deprioritize.

name: Trans Bus Inc
fleet_size_est: unknown (residential address at 15516 Old Pond Cir; FreeFreightSearch lists as freight broker #27313 — suggests motor-coach/charter broker or small operator, NOT a school bus contractor)
service_types: unclear; freight-broker directory listing and residential HQ inconsistent with school bus contractor profile; not listed in Noblesville Schools transportation vendors
districts_or_customers: None confirmed; Noblesville Schools operates its own in-house transportation
recent_news: No M&A news; no operator footprint identified
fit_notes: Likely NOT an independent yellow-bus operator. Residential HQ address + freight-broker directory listing suggests this is a small charter/broker entity or a misclassified record. Skip for Summit tuck-in pipeline unless direct outreach confirms school-bus operations.

name: Empire State Bus Corp
owner_or_parent: Curcio family group (operated as single employer with Allied Transit Corp and Empire Charter Service Inc per NLRB)
principals: Valerie Curcio (owner of Allied Transit); John Curcio (signs on behalf of group)
fleet_size_est: unknown for Empire State alone; combined Curcio group operates substantial NYC fleet (Allied Transit deployed $7.6M for Canarsie yard 2025)
employees_est: 49 attributed to Empire State entity; group total larger
service_types: school (NYC DOE OPT contractor); charter (Empire Charter Service sister entity)
districts_or_customers: NYC DOE (Brooklyn, Staten Island routes); Staten Island yard at 101 Shale St; main Brooklyn ops at 141 Hinsdale St / 148 Snediker Ave / 12505 Essex St
recent_news: June 2025: affiliate Allied Transit (via Monk Holdings Corp) acquired Canarsie garage and lot for $7.6M, signaling expansion/reinvestment; Sept 2020 NLRB Local 854 won representation election across the 3-entity group
fit_notes: Empire State Bus Corp is one leg of the Curcio family's 3-entity NYC school bus group (Allied Transit / Empire State / Empire Charter — single employer per NLRB). Recent $7.6M garage purchase signals reinvestment, not exit. Could be a multi-entity Curcio family acquisition opportunity at group level for Summit, but Empire State alone is unlikely sold separately. Family-owned, NYC-concentrated — fits NYC consolidation thesis if approached at family/group level.
